title: Chewy Q4 2024 Earnings Call: Customer Growth, AutoShip, and Vet Care Expansion Drive Results
text: Chewy's Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted three key drivers of results: renewed active customer growth, continued autoship strength, and strategic vet care expansion. Management emphasized the return to active customer growth after eight quarters of declines, with the 2.1% increase signaling inflection in customer acquisition and retention. Autoship's 21% growth in Q4 and 10.6% full-year growth significantly outpaced overall revenue growth, reinforcing the recurring revenue model's value. Eight vet care clinics opened in 2024, with plans for 8-10 additional in 2025, positioning the company for high-margin health ecosystem expansion. Free cash flow generation of $452.5 million demonstrated the business model's cash conversion capability. Management guidance for 6-7% revenue growth in 2025 assumes continued market share gains despite normalizing industry growth. Full-year adjusted EBITDA margin guidance of 5.4-5.7% implies continued margin expansion from both gross margin improvements (premium categories, sponsored ads, health) and SG&A leverage. The combination of customer growth inflection, autoship momentum, and vet care traction positioned Chewy for accelerating profitability in 2025.
